what: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: goal pTRAPPING provide user-friendly, reproducible, customizable workflow analysis TRAP-seq data R. package includes functions differential expression analysis, visualization, functional enrichment, designed work seamlessly together.

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: can install development version pTRAPPING GitHub :

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R

what: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: Runs complete limma workflow feature--sample expression abundance matrix: builds design matrix group vector, fits linear model, applies contrasts, tests differential expression using either limma::treat() limma::eBayes(). Returns list containing per-contrast DE tables, combined table significant features, summary features inconsistent directions across contrasts, direction counts.

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
code: limma_de(   expr_mat,   group,   contrast_mat,   coefs = NULL,   model_type = c("pairwise", "reference"),   test_method = c("treat", "eBayes"),   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   feature_col = "feature",   ngenes_out = 20,   kable_out = FALSE,   kable_coef = 1 )

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: expr_mat numeric matrix features (genes / proteins) rows samples columns. Row names must feature identifiers column names must sample IDs. group character factor vector group labels, one element per column expr_mat. order must match columns expr_mat. contrast_mat contrast matrix produced limma::makeContrasts(), character vector contrast strings (e.g. c("groupA-groupB", "groupB-groupC")) passed limma::makeContrasts() internally. Contrast names must match column names design matrix (e.g. "groupA", "groupB", ...). coefs integer vector specifying columns contrast_mat extract results . NULL (default), contrasts used. model_type One "pairwise" (intercept, ~ 0 + group, recommended multi-group designs explicit contrasts) "reference" (first level reference, ~ group). See Choosing model_type section. Default "pairwise". test_method One "treat" "eBayes". See Choosing test_method section guidance. Default "treat". lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change. Passed treat(lfc = lfc_threshold) test_method = "treat", used filter threshold methods. Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum adjusted p-value feature classified differentially expressed. Default 0.05. feature_col Name column hold feature identifiers (.e. row names expr_mat) output tables. Default "feature". ngenes_out Number top features display kable_out = TRUE. Default 20. kable_out Logical. TRUE, returns HTML kableExtra table single contrast (indexed kable_coef) instead full list. Default FALSE. kable_coef Integer giving position within coefs contrast display kable_out = TRUE. Default 1 (first selected contrast).

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: kable_out = FALSE (default), named list : de_list named list tibbles, one per selected contrast, containing DE features (filtered lfc_threshold fdr_threshold). tibble includes feature_col, logFC, adj.P.Val, contrast, diffexpressed ("" ""). all_de per-contrast DE tibbles de_list combined one tibble dplyr::bind_rows(). feature appears per contrast significant. shared Tibble features DE opposite directions across different contrasts (min logFC < 0 max logFC > 0). Useful flagging features whose direction change consistent. unique Tibble counting consistently DE features direction ("" "") — features absent shared. fit fitted MArrayLM object returned treat() eBayes(), downstream use (e.g. calling topTreat() topTable() additional contrast columns). kable_out = TRUE, HTML kableExtra table top ngenes_out features selected contrast.

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: "pairwise" Fits model.matrix(~ 0 + group). intercept; every group gets coefficient. Use want explicit pairwise contrasts (e.g. groupA - groupB). Requires contrast matrix. "reference" Fits model.matrix(~ group). first factor level intercept / reference group; coefficients represent differences . Simpler one natural reference condition comparisons.

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: "treat" Calls limma::treat(), tests minimum fold-change threshold (lfc_threshold) rather zero. Recommended proteomics metabolomics data want ensure biological well statistical significance. Results extracted limma::topTreat(). "eBayes" Calls limma::eBayes(), standard empirical Bayes moderation. Tests null logFC = 0. Appropriate RNA-seq (voom) analysis minimum fold-change required. Results extracted limma::topTable().

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # --- pairwise design with treat() (proteomics / metabolomics) --- group_vec <- rep(c("groupA", "groupB", "groupC"), each = 3)  # build the contrast matrix for selected pairwise comparisons df_design <- data.frame(group = factor(group_vec)) design_mat <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group, data = df_design) cont_mat <- limma::makeContrasts(   groupA - groupB,   groupA - groupC,   levels = design_mat )  res <- limma_de(   expr_mat      = my_matrix,   group         = group_vec,   contrast_mat  = cont_mat,   coefs         = 1:2,   test_method   = "treat",   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05 )  res$de_list    # named list, one tibble per contrast res$all_de     # all contrasts combined res$shared  # features with inconsistent direction res$unique  # count of consistently UP / DOWN features  # --- single comparison, reference group design with eBayes() --- res2 <- limma_de(   expr_mat     = my_matrix,   group        = group_vec,   contrast_mat = "groupgroupB",   # coefficient name in ~ group design   model_type   = "reference",   test_method  = "eBayes" ) } # }

what: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR — ptrap_de
title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR — ptrap_de
text: Compares IP vs INPUT fractions specified brain region treatment condition using edgeR's GLM framework. Accounts paired structure via block variable (e.g., individual animal tube). Supports likelihood ratio test (glmLRT) quasi-likelihood F-test (glmQLFTest) final hypothesis testing step. Returns tibble differential expression results. Optionally returns kableExtra HTML table top DE genes.

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR — ptrap_de
code: ptrap_de(   counts_mat,   sample_df,   gene_ids,   region_name,   treatment_name,   sample_col = "sample",   fraction_col = "fraction",   block_col = "tube",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   treatment_col = "Treatment",   ip_level = "IP",   input_level = "INPUT",   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   test_method = c("LRT", "QLF"),   ngenes.out = 20,   kable.out = FALSE )

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR — ptrap_de
text: counts_mat numeric matrix raw counts genes rows samples columns. Column names must match values sample_col. sample_df data frame containing sample metadata. Must include columns sample names, IP/INPUT fraction, block variable, brain region, treatment condition. gene_ids character vector gene identifiers corresponding rows counts_mat. region_name brain region subset analyze (e.g., "POA"). Must match value region_col. treatment_name treatment condition subset analyze (e.g., "pb"). Must match value treatment_col. sample_col Name column sample_df whose values match column names counts_mat. Default "sample". fraction_col Name column sample_df distinguishes IP INPUT fractions. Default "fraction". block_col Name column sample_df used blocking / pairing variable design matrix (e.g., individual animal tube). Default "tube". region_col Name column sample_df containing brain region labels. Default "BrainRegion". treatment_col Name column sample_df containing treatment labels. Default "Treatment". ip_level value fraction_col identifies IP fraction. Default "IP". input_level value fraction_col identifies INPUT fraction (used reference level). Default "INPUT". lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change required classify gene differentially expressed. Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum FDR allowed classify gene differentially expressed. Default 0.05. test_method GLM testing method use. Either "LRT" (likelihood ratio test via glmFit + glmLRT, default) "QLF" (quasi-likelihood F-test via glmQLFit + glmQLFTest). "QLF" generally conservative recommended number samples per group small. ngenes.Number top genes (sorted p-value) include output kable.= TRUE. Default 20. kable.Logical. TRUE, returns kableExtra HTML table top ngenes.genes instead full tibble. Requires kableExtra package. Default FALSE.

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR — ptrap_de
text: kable.= FALSE (default), tibble one row per gene, sorted p-value. kable.= TRUE, HTML kableExtra table top ngenes.genes. tibble contains: Gene Gene identifier gene_ids. logFC Log2 fold change (IP vs INPUT). logCPM Average log2 counts per million. LR / F Test statistic (name depends test_method). PValue Raw p-value. FDR Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p-value. BrainRegion / region_col Brain region label. Treatment / treatment_col Treatment label. diffexpressed "", "", "" based lfc_threshold fdr_threshold.

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR — ptrap_de
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default call using LRT res_lrt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb" )  # Using the quasi-likelihood F-test instead res_qlf <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "QLF" )  # Custom thresholds res_custom <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   lfc_threshold  = 0.5,   fdr_threshold  = 0.1 ) } # }

what: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: Takes single tibble produced ptrap_de(), limma_de() similar returns classic volcano plot (logFC x-axis, -log10(FDR) y-axis). Non-significant genes shown grey; genes classified "" "" diffexpressed column highlighted distinct fill colours labelled gene identifier. Dashed threshold lines drawn ±lfc_threshold (vertical) -log10(fdr_threshold) (horizontal).

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: ptrap_volcano(   de_result,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "Treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL )

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: de_result tibble returned ptrap_de(), containing minimum columns Gene (gene_col), logFC, FDR, diffexpressed. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change used draw vertical threshold lines. match value used ptrap_de(). Default 1. fdr_threshold FDR cutoff used draw horizontal threshold line (plotted -log10(fdr_threshold)). match value used ptrap_de(). Default 0.05. gene_col Name column containing gene identifiers. Default "Gene". treatment_col Name column containing treatment label. Used build default plot title. Default "Treatment". region_col Name column containing brain region label. Used build default plot title. Default "BrainRegion". colors named character vector mapping "" "" colours. NULL (default), colourblind-friendly palette used ("#D55E00" "", "#0072B2" ""). point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity highlighted (significant) points. Default 0.7. max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Controls many overlapping labels suppressed. Default 20. title Plot title. NULL (default), title auto-generated brain region treatment labels found de_result.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb" )  # Default plot ptrap_volcano(res)  # Custom thresholds and colours ptrap_volcano(res,               lfc_threshold = 0.5,               fdr_threshold = 0.1,               colors        = c("UP" = "#E69F00", "DOWN" = "#56B4E9")) } # }

what: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: Takes two tibbles produced ptrap_de() similar — one per treatment condition, brain region — joins gene, classifies gene according differential expression status condition, returns scatter plot logFC\(_{\text{treatment 1}}\) vs logFC\(_{\text{treatment 2}}\). Significant genes highlighted labelled; threshold lines drawn ±lfc_threshold axes.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
code: ptrap_volcano2(   de_result_1,   de_result_2,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "Treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL )

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: de_result_1 tibble returned ptrap_de() first treatment condition (plotted y-axis). de_result_2 tibble returned ptrap_de() second treatment condition (plotted x-axis). lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change used define significance. Must match (stricter ) threshold used ptrap_de(). Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum FDR used define significance. Default 0.05. gene_col Name column containing gene identifiers result tibbles. Default "Gene". treatment_col Name column containing treatment label result tibbles. Used auto-generate axis labels DE class names. Default "Treatment". region_col Name column containing brain region label. Used set default plot title. Default "BrainRegion". colors named character vector mapping DE class colour. Names must "DE ", "DE <t1>", "DE <t2>", <t1> / <t2> treatment names found data. NULL (default), colourblind-friendly palette used. point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity coloured (significant) points. Default 0.7. max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Controls many overlapping labels allowed hidden. Default 20. title Plot title. NULL (default), brain region name extracted de_result_1 used.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res_pb  <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "pb") res_sol <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "sol")  # Default plot — treatment 1 on y-axis, treatment 2 on x-axis ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol)  # Custom thresholds, title and colours ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol,                lfc_threshold = 0.5,                fdr_threshold = 0.1,                title         = "POA: pb vs sol",                colors        = c("DE in both"  = "#D55E00",                                  "DE only pb"  = "#0072B2",                                  "DE only sol" = "#49a15f")) } # }
